title: TLS 1.3, Certificate Identity, ALPN, and HTTPS
description: A service enables TLS without certificate-name validation, chooses HTTP after reading encrypted application bytes, allows unsafe versions, and treats encryption as authorization and content integrity proof. Learners cumulatively produce TLS identity, negotiation, and protocol boundary record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish TCP connection, TLS records, handshake, authenticated peer identity, traffic keys, alerts, application data, and closure.
Validate certificate chain, time, DNS or IP reference identity, key usage, revocation policy, and trust roots without insecure skip flags.
Offer and select application protocols with ALPN before application data and reject unsupported or inconsistent negotiation.
